question: [File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / repay()] Can an unprivileged borrower using a fee-on-transfer loan token call repay(market, units, onBehalf, address(0), '') under the precondition that the loan token deducts a fee on every transferFrom, causing the sequence: (1) position[id][onBehalf].debt -= units and marketState[id].withdrawable += units are committed, (2) safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), units) transfers only units*(1-fee_rate) to Midnight, violating the invariant that marketState[id].withdrawable <= actual loanToken balance of Midnight, causing scoped impact: insolvency where lenders collectively cannot withdraw their full credit because withdrawable exceeds actual token balance? Proof idea: deploy fee-on-transfer ERC20 with 1% fee, create market, have borrower repay N units, assert Midnight.balanceOf(loanToken) < marketState[id].withdrawable after repay.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / supplyCollateral()] Can an unprivileged borrower using a fee-on-transfer collateral token call supplyCollateral(market, collateralIndex, assets, onBehalf) under the precondition that the collateral token deducts a fee on transferFrom, causing the sequence: (1) position[id][onBehalf].collateral[collateralIndex] += assets is committed with the full nominal amount, (2) safeTransferFrom(collateralToken, msg.sender, address(this), assets) delivers only assets*(1-fee_rate) to Midnight, violating the invariant that position[id][borrower].collateral[i] <= actual collateralToken balance held by Midnight for that market, causing scoped impact: bad debt where borrower borrows against phantom collateral that does not exist in the contract? Proof idea: fuzz fee_rate in [1,50]%, supply collateral, take borrow offer, assert Midnight collateral balance < sum of all position[id][*].collateral[collateralIndex].

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / take()] Can an unprivileged taker using a fee-on-transfer loan token call take(offer, ratifierData, units, taker, receiver, address(0), '') under the precondition that the loan token charges a fee on transferFrom and the offer is a buy offer (offer.buy == true), causing the sequence: (1) claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] += buyerAssets - sellerAssets is committed, (2) safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), buyerAssets - sellerAssets) delivers only (buyerAssets-sellerAssets)*(1-fee_rate) to Midnight, violating the invariant that claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] <= actual loanToken balance reserved for fees, causing scoped impact: insolvency where fee claimer drains lender withdrawable funds when claiming overcounted settlement fees? Proof idea: deploy fee-on-transfer token, execute take, assert claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] > actual fee tokens received, then call claimSettlementFee and assert lender withdraw fails.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / take() dual-transfer path] Can an unprivileged taker using a fee-on-transfer loan token call take(offer, ratifierData, units, taker, receiver, address(0), '') under the precondition that the loan token charges a fee on every transferFrom and the offer is a sell offer (offer.buy == false), causing the sequence: (1) safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), buyerAssets - sellerAssets) is called, (2) safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, receiver, sellerAssets) is called, (3) receiver (maker's receiverIfMakerIsSeller) receives only sellerAssets*(1-fee_rate) instead of sellerAssets, violating the invariant that the maker receives exactly sellerAssets loan tokens for their debt obligation, causing scoped impact: direct loss where the maker-as-seller is systematically underpaid relative to their debt position? Proof idea: fuzz sellerAssets and fee_rate, assert receiver.balanceOf(loanToken) after take == sellerAssets*(1-fee_rate) < sellerAssets.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / liquidate() loan token repayment] Can an unprivileged liquidator using a fee-on-transfer loan token call liquidate(market, collateralIndex, 0, repaidUnits, borrower, false, receiver, address(0), '') under the precondition that the loan token charges a fee on transferFrom, causing the sequence: (1) marketState[id].withdrawable += repaidUnits is committed, (2) position[id][borrower].debt -= repaidUnits is committed, (3) safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), repaidUnits) delivers only repaidUnits*(1-fee_rate) to Midnight, violating the invariant that marketState[id].withdrawable <= actual loanToken balance of Midnight, causing scoped impact: insolvency where lenders cannot all redeem their credit because withdrawable exceeds actual balance? Proof idea: liquidate with fee-on-transfer loan token, assert Midnight.balanceOf(loanToken) < marketState[id].withdrawable post-liquidation.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer / liquidate() collateral seizure] Can an unprivileged liquidator using a fee-on-transfer collateral token call liquidate(market, collateralIndex, seizedAssets, 0, borrower, false, receiver, address(0), '') under the precondition that the collateral token charges a fee on transfer, causing the sequence: (1) position[id][borrower].collateral[collateralIndex] -= seizedAssets is committed, (2) safeTransfer(collateralToken, receiver, seizedAssets) delivers only seizedAssets*(1-fee_rate) to the liquidator, violating the invariant that the liquidator receives the full LIF-discounted collateral value they are entitled to, causing scoped impact: liquidation failure where the effective liquidation incentive is reduced below profitability, leaving unhealthy positions permanently unliquidatable? Proof idea: fuzz fee_rate and LIF, find threshold where seizedAssets*(1-fee_rate)*price < repaidUnits, assert liquidation becomes economically irrational.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer / withdraw()] Can an unprivileged lender using a fee-on-transfer loan token call withdraw(market, units, onBehalf, receiver) under the precondition that the loan token charges a fee on transfer, causing the sequence: (1) position[id][onBehalf].credit -= units, marketState[id].withdrawable -= units, marketState[id].totalUnits -= units are committed, (2) safeTransfer(loanToken, receiver, units) delivers only units*(1-fee_rate) to receiver, violating the invariant that a lender withdrawing units credit receives exactly units loan tokens, causing scoped impact: direct loss where lenders systematically receive less than their credit entitlement on every withdrawal? Proof idea: deploy fee-on-transfer token, lend N units, withdraw N units, assert receiver balance == N*(1-fee_rate) < N.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer / withdrawCollateral()] Can an unprivileged borrower using a fee-on-transfer collateral token call withdrawCollateral(market, collateralIndex, assets, onBehalf, receiver) under the precondition that the collateral token charges a fee on transfer and the borrower passes the health check, causing the sequence: (1) position[id][onBehalf].collateral[collateralIndex] -= assets is committed, (2) isHealthy check passes, (3) safeTransfer(collateralToken, receiver, assets) delivers only assets*(1-fee_rate) to receiver, violating the invariant that a borrower withdrawing assets collateral receives exactly assets tokens, causing scoped impact: direct loss where borrowers systematically lose a fee percentage of their collateral on every withdrawal? Proof idea: supply 1000 tokens of fee-on-transfer collateral, withdraw 1000, assert receiver balance == 1000*(1-fee_rate).

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer / claimSettlementFee()] Can an unprivileged fee claimer using a fee-on-transfer loan token call claimSettlementFee(token, amount, receiver) under the precondition that the loan token charges a fee on transfer, causing the sequence: (1) claimableSettlementFee[token] -= amount is committed, (2) safeTransfer(token, receiver, amount) delivers only amount*(1-fee_rate) to receiver, violating the invariant that the fee claimer receives exactly the amount of fees they claim, causing scoped impact: direct loss where the fee claimer receives less than the claimed amount, and if claimableSettlementFee was already at the correct balance, the protocol's loanToken balance is now short by amount*fee_rate? Proof idea: accumulate settlement fees, claim with fee-on-transfer token, assert receiver balance < claimed amount and protocol balance desync.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer / claimContinuousFee()] Can an unprivileged fee claimer using a fee-on-transfer loan token call claimContinuousFee(market, amount, receiver) under the precondition that the loan token charges a fee on transfer, causing the sequence: (1) marketState[id].continuousFeeCredit -= amount, marketState[id].totalUnits -= amount, marketState[id].withdrawable -= amount are committed, (2) safeTransfer(loanToken, receiver, amount) delivers only amount*(1-fee_rate) to receiver, violating the invariant that the fee claimer receives exactly amount loan tokens, causing scoped impact: direct loss to fee claimer and accounting desync where withdrawable is reduced by amount but only amount*(1-fee_rate) left the contract? Proof idea: accrue continuous fees, claim with fee-on-transfer token, assert receiver balance < amount and Midnight loanToken balance > withdrawable.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer / flashLoan()] Can an unprivileged flash loan user using a fee-on-transfer loan token call flashLoan([loanToken], [assets], callback, data) under the precondition that the loan token charges a fee on both transfer and transferFrom, causing the sequence: (1) safeTransfer(loanToken, callback, assets) delivers only assets*(1-fee_rate) to callback, (2) callback receives less than assets, (3) safeTransferFrom(loanToken, callback, address(this), assets) pulls assets from callback but Midnight receives only assets*(1-fee_rate), violating the invariant that flashLoan repayment restores Midnight's balance to pre-loan levels, causing scoped impact: insolvency where each flash loan permanently drains assets*fee_rate from the protocol? Proof idea: execute flash loan with fee-on-transfer token, assert Midnight balance after < Midnight balance before.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / supplyCollateral() + isHealthy() cross-module] Can an unprivileged borrower using a fee-on-transfer collateral token execute the sequence: (1) supplyCollateral(market, 0, assets, borrower) where collateral token has 10% fee, recording position[id][borrower].collateral[0] = assets but Midnight receives assets*0.9, (2) take sell offer to borrow against the full nominal collateral value, (3) isHealthy() computes maxDebt using position[id][borrower].collateral[0] = assets (not actual 0.9*assets), under the precondition that oracle price and LLTV are set such that assets*price*LLTV/WAD >= debt but 0.9*assets*price*LLTV/WAD < debt, violating the invariant that every debt position is backed by sufficient actual collateral held by the protocol, causing scoped impact: bad debt creation where borrower is undercollateralized relative to actual token holdings? Proof idea: fuzz fee_rate and LLTV, find parameters where phantom collateral enables undercollateralized borrowing.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / supplyCollateral() + liquidate() cross-module] Can an unprivileged borrower using a fee-on-transfer collateral token execute the sequence: (1) supplyCollateral with fee-on-transfer collateral creating phantom collateral, (2) borrow against phantom collateral, (3) position becomes unhealthy, (4) liquidator calls liquidate() and seizes collateral, but safeTransfer(collateralToken, receiver, seizedAssets) delivers only seizedAssets*(1-fee_rate) while repaidUnits is computed from the full seizedAssets value, under the precondition that fee_rate is large enough to make the liquidation unprofitable, violating the invariant that liquidations must remain economically viable to maintain protocol solvency, causing scoped impact: liquidation failure where bad debt from phantom collateral cannot be cleared? Proof idea: invariant test asserting that for any fee_rate > 0, there exists a position where liquidation is unprofitable.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / repay() + withdraw() insolvency cascade] Can an unprivileged borrower using a fee-on-transfer loan token execute the sequence: (1) multiple borrowers repay their debt via repay(), each time marketState[id].withdrawable increases by units but Midnight receives units*(1-fee_rate), (2) lenders call withdraw() expecting to receive their full credit, (3) the last lenders to withdraw find Midnight's loanToken balance insufficient, under the precondition that N borrowers each repay units_i and the total fee paid is sum(units_i * fee_rate), violating the invariant that sum of all lender credits <= Midnight loanToken balance, causing scoped impact: insolvency cascade where early withdrawers drain the pool and late withdrawers are frozen? Proof idea: stateful fuzz with N borrowers repaying and N lenders withdrawing, assert final lender always fails.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: SafeTransferLib / multicall() + fee-on-transfer] Can an unprivileged user using a fee-on-transfer loan token call multicall([repay_calldata, withdraw_calldata]) under the precondition that the loan token charges a fee on every transfer, causing the sequence: (1) repay() increases withdrawable by units but receives units*(1-fee_rate), (2) withdraw() decreases withdrawable by units and sends units to receiver, (3) receiver receives units but Midnight only received units*(1-fee_rate) from repay, violating the invariant that Midnight's loanToken balance never goes below zero, causing scoped impact: insolvency where multicall allows atomic extraction of more tokens than were deposited? Proof idea: multicall repay+withdraw in same tx with fee-on-transfer token, assert Midnight balance decreases by more than net deposit.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer / withdraw() + fee-on-transfer + bad debt socialization] Can an unprivileged attacker using a fee-on-transfer loan token execute the sequence: (1) create market with fee-on-transfer loan token, (2) multiple repayments cause withdrawable > actual balance by delta = sum(units_i * fee_rate), (3) bad debt is realized via liquidate() which further reduces totalUnits but not the existing balance shortfall, (4) lenders call updatePosition() which slashes their credit proportionally, (5) lenders attempt withdraw() but Midnight balance is insufficient, under the precondition that both fee-on-transfer and bad debt occur in the same market, violating the invariant that after bad debt socialization all remaining lender credits are redeemable, causing scoped impact: insolvency amplification where fee-on-transfer shortfall compounds with bad debt loss? Proof idea: invariant test combining fee-on-transfer repayments and bad debt liquidations, assert sum(credit_i) <= Midnight.balanceOf(loanToken).

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer / token returning false on transfer] Can an unprivileged lender be permanently frozen when a loan token that returns false on transfer is used, under the precondition that the loan token's transfer() returns false (not reverts) for any reason (e.g., blacklist, pause), causing the sequence: (1) lender calls withdraw(market, units, onBehalf, receiver), (2) position[id][onBehalf].credit -= units and marketState[id].withdrawable -= units are committed, (3) safeTransfer(loanToken, receiver, units) calls transfer() which returns false, (4) SafeTransferLib reverts with TransferReturnedFalse(), (5) all state changes revert, (6) lender retries but token continues returning false, violating the invariant that a lender with valid credit can always withdraw their funds, causing scoped impact: fund freeze where lender credit is permanently inaccessible? Proof idea: deploy token with toggleable false-return, toggle off, assert withdraw always reverts.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / token returning false on transferFrom in repay()] Can an unprivileged borrower be permanently unable to repay when a loan token returns false on transferFrom, under the precondition that the loan token's transferFrom() returns false for the borrower (e.g., allowance revoked, blacklist), causing the sequence: (1) borrower calls repay(market, units, onBehalf, address(0), ''), (2) position[id][onBehalf].debt -= units and marketState[id].withdrawable += units are committed, (3) safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), units) returns false, (4) SafeTransferLib reverts with TransferFromReturnedFalse(), (5) state reverts, (6) borrower cannot repay, (7) position accrues to maturity and becomes liquidatable, violating the invariant that a borrower can always repay their debt, causing scoped impact: fund freeze and forced liquidation of a borrower who cannot repay? Proof idea: deploy token with per-address false-return toggle, assert repay always reverts for targeted borrower.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / token returning true on failed transfer (silent failure)] Can an unprivileged borrower using a token that returns true even when transfer fails (e.g., insufficient balance, no actual transfer) call repay(market, units, onBehalf, address(0), '') under the precondition that the loan token returns true on transferFrom regardless of success, causing the sequence: (1) position[id][onBehalf].debt -= units is committed, (2) marketState[id].withdrawable += units is committed, (3) safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), units) returns true but transfers 0 tokens, (4) SafeTransferLib does not revert (returndata.length > 0 && abi.decode(returndata, (bool)) == true), violating the invariant that debt reduction must be matched by actual loan token receipt, causing scoped impact: insolvency where withdrawable increases without corresponding token receipt, allowing lenders to drain the pool? Proof idea: deploy always-true-return token, repay without holding tokens, assert Midnight balance unchanged but withdrawable increased.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / token returning true on failed transferFrom in supplyCollateral()] Can an unprivileged borrower using a token that silently fails (returns true but transfers nothing) call supplyCollateral(market, collateralIndex, assets, onBehalf) under the precondition that the collateral token returns true on transferFrom without transferring, causing the sequence: (1) position[id][onBehalf].collateral[collateralIndex] += assets is committed, (2) collateralBitmap is updated, (3) safeTransferFrom(collateralToken, msg.sender, address(this), assets) returns true but transfers 0, (4) borrower now has phantom collateral, (5) borrower takes a sell offer to borrow against phantom collateral, violating the invariant that collateral accounting reflects actual token holdings, causing scoped impact: bad debt creation where borrower borrows with zero actual collateral? Proof idea: deploy silent-success token, supply collateral, borrow, assert Midnight collateral balance == 0 but position.collateral[i] > 0.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: SafeTransferLib / token returning non-standard returndata] Can an unprivileged market creator deploy a market with a loan token that returns non-bool returndata (e.g., 64 bytes, or a bytes32 value) from transfer/transferFrom, under the precondition that the token returns 64 bytes where the first 32 bytes are zero (e.g., abi.encode(uint256(0), uint256(1))), causing the sequence: (1) safeTransfer or safeTransferFrom is called, (2) returndata.length == 64 != 0, (3) abi.decode(returndata, (bool)) decodes first 32 bytes as bool(0) == false, (4) SafeTransferLib reverts with TransferReturnedFalse() or TransferFromReturnedFalse(), violating the invariant that a successful token transfer does not cause protocol functions to revert, causing scoped impact: fund freeze where all withdrawals, repayments, and liquidations revert for this token? Proof idea: deploy token returning abi.encode(0, 1) from transfer, assert withdraw always reverts with TransferReturnedFalse.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: SafeTransferLib / token reverting on zero-value transfer in take()] Can an unprivileged taker using a loan token that reverts on zero-value transfers call take(offer, ratifierData, units, taker, receiver, address(0), '') under the precondition that the settlement fee is zero (settlementFee(id, timeToMaturity) == 0, so buyerAssets == sellerAssets), causing the sequence: (1) all position and consumed state updates are committed, (2) safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), buyerAssets - sellerAssets) is called with value 0, (3) token reverts on zero transfer, (4) entire take() reverts, violating the invariant that take() should succeed when all offer conditions are met and the taker has sufficient funds, causing scoped impact: low-cost DoS where any market with zero settlement fee and a zero-revert token is permanently unusable? Proof idea: deploy zero-revert token, create market with zero settlement fee, attempt take, assert always reverts.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: SafeTransferLib / ERC777 tokensToSend reentrancy in supplyCollateral()] Can an unprivileged borrower using an ERC777-like collateral token (with tokensToSend hook) call supplyCollateral(market, collateralIndex, assets, onBehalf) under the precondition that the collateral token triggers a tokensToSend hook on the sender during transferFrom, causing the sequence: (1) position[id][onBehalf].collateral[collateralIndex] += assets is committed, (2) collateralBitmap is updated, (3) safeTransferFrom(collateralToken, msg.sender, address(this), assets) triggers tokensToSend on msg.sender, (4) msg.sender reenters withdrawCollateral(market, collateralIndex, assets, onBehalf, receiver) which passes isHealthy() because collateral was already credited, (5) collateral is withdrawn before the outer transferFrom completes, violating the invariant that collateral accounting must match actual token holdings, causing scoped impact: unauthorized collateral withdrawal where borrower withdraws collateral that was never actually deposited? Proof idea: implement ERC777 tokensToSend hook that reenters withdrawCollateral, assert borrower extracts collateral with zero net deposit.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: SafeTransferLib / ERC777 tokensReceived reentrancy in withdrawCollateral()] Can an unprivileged borrower using an ERC777-like collateral token (with tokensReceived hook) call withdrawCollateral(market, collateralIndex, assets, onBehalf, receiver) under the precondition that the collateral token triggers a tokensReceived hook on the receiver during transfer, causing the sequence: (1) position[id][onBehalf].collateral[collateralIndex] -= assets is committed, (2) isHealthy() passes, (3) safeTransfer(collateralToken, receiver, assets) triggers tokensReceived on receiver, (4) receiver reenters withdrawCollateral() again for the same collateralIndex, (5) position.collateral[collateralIndex] is now 0 so the second withdrawal reverts with underflow, but if receiver reenters a different collateralIndex or a different function, state may be exploitable, violating the invariant that no reentrancy path can extract more collateral than deposited, causing scoped impact: accounting corruption or unauthorized collateral extraction? Proof idea: implement tokensReceived hook that reenters protocol, fuzz reentry targets, assert collateral balance invariant.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: SafeTransferLib / ERC777 tokensReceived reentrancy in withdraw()] Can an unprivileged lender using an ERC777-like loan token (with tokensReceived hook) call withdraw(market, units, onBehalf, receiver) under the precondition that the loan token triggers a tokensReceived hook on the receiver during transfer, causing the sequence: (1) position[id][onBehalf].credit -= units, marketState[id].withdrawable -= units, marketState[id].totalUnits -= units are committed, (2) safeTransfer(loanToken, receiver, units) triggers tokensReceived on receiver, (3) receiver reenters withdraw() for the same or different position, (4) if receiver is authorized for another user's position, they can drain additional credit, violating the invariant that a single withdraw call reduces credit by exactly units and no more, causing scoped impact: unauthorized withdrawal where reentrancy allows draining credit beyond the caller's entitlement? Proof idea: implement tokensReceived hook that reenters withdraw for a second authorized position, assert total withdrawn > initial credit.
